Chinese Term: 万法归宗，万教归一，天下一家，世界大同，是新时代的主旋律。
Standard English (Chanyuan Style):
All paths and teachings ultimately return to one source—the Greatest Creator.
All religions, beliefs, and schools of thought converge toward a shared recognition of the Greatest Creator as the common origin of LIFE.
The world as one family, and humanity living in harmony—this is the main theme of the New Era.

All paths and teachings return to one source.
All religions and philosophies converge toward the Greatest Creator.
One world, one family—harmony in the New Era.
the greatest form has no form; non-action leaves nothing undone
the highest virtue claims no virtue; the greatest mercy shows no mercy
misguided good, limited good, and the greater good
the turning point of human civilization
Tao, Virtue, Law, Benevolence, Righteousness, Propriety, Wisdom, Integrity, Method
Karmic reward and retribution are exact to the slightest degree.
all under Heaven as one family
Heaven’s net is vast; its mesh is wide, yet nothing escapes.

Chinese Term: 法无定法，无法是法，形无定形，无形是形。
Standard English (Chanyuan Style):
There is no fixed method; what has no fixed method is the method.
There is no fixed form; formlessness is form.

Chinese Term: 人分浑人、俗人、凡人、贤人、圣人五种
Standard English (Chanyuan Style):
People fall into five categories:
(1)   Ignorant people
(2)   Worldly people
(3)   Mortals
(4)   Sages
(5)   Saints
Blessing and calamity have no fixed gate; they are summoned by oneself.
the origin from the incarnation of the dragon
the origin from the Garden of Eden

Chinese Term: 万般皆游戏，唯有性命真
Standard English (Chanyuan Style): All things are but games; only LIFE is real and eternal.

Chinese Term: 下智躁动，中智竞争，大智若愚，上智无形
Standard English (Chanyuan Style): Lower wisdom is restless; middle wisdom competes; great wisdom seems simple; highest wisdom is formless.

Chinese Term: 不易中变易，变易中不易
Standard English (Chanyuan Style): Within change, there is the unchanging; within the unchanging, there is change.
nation, political party, religion, and family

Chinese Term: 行于是，止于是，造次于是，逍遥于是
Category: being present in action and rest, at ease in all moments

Notes / Usage Rules: Fixed, never God

Standard English (Chanyuan Style): the Rulai Primordial Buddha (the Greatest Creator)
Rulai （ the original suchness of the universe ）/ Rulai (rendered as “Tathagata” in Buddhist texts)仅在第一次解释用
the Way of the Greatest Creator
the consciousness of the Greatest Creator
the Kingdom of the Greatest Creator

Buddha-dharma（首选，体系用语）
the Dharma of the Buddha（解释性）
Buddhist teachings（世俗/说明性，可用但不作核心术语）
The Buddha-dharma is one and indivisible, yet manifests in countless forms.
The Buddha-dharma has no fixed form, and thus gives rise to endless transformations.
to attain Celestial Being through practice
to become a Celestial Being or attain Buddhahood
The Three Realms are mind-only; all phenomena arise from consciousness.

Notes / Usage Rules: Realm / state, not a planet

Notes / Usage Rules: Zero World; connected to all parallel worlds
